note: Sprint 5.25 Trinity Governor Watchdog v0.1: adds scripts/trinity/governor_watchdog.py as the missing external eye on the Autonomy Governor audit trail. The Watchdog is a separate process that reads TRINITY_AUTONOMY_GOVERNOR_DECISION_*.json files from a directory and emits a deterministic report JSON (schemas/trinity/governor_watchdog_report.schema.json, $id=trinity-governor-watchdog-report/v0.1) summarising allowed/blocked/malformed counts, halt detection, policy mutation detection, threat refs seen, actions seen, decision ids and an overall safety_status. Four safety_status values with strict precedence: critical (halt_file_present or policy_mutated_at_runtime detected) > warning (malformed decision or policy_hashes_match=false) > stale (newest decision older than max_age_seconds, default 3600) > ok. The Governor itself remains network-free; the Watchdog is the first Trinity component allowed network egress but v0.1 ships with the surface gated and not exercised — --webhook-url and --send are wired but the scan source contains zero network primitives (urlopen, urllib.request, requests.post, http.client, socket.socket), all verified by a static grep test. Webhook URL is host-redacted in the report (scheme://host only, path/query stripped). webhook_sent is locked const false at the schema level so any future code change that flips it without bumping the schema fails CI. Watchdog is read-only on the decisions dir: static safety test rejects .unlink, .chmod, os.remove, shutil.rmtree, os.rename in the source. Refuses to look at any directory whose basename or path segment matches a denylist (wallets, secrets, keys, private, .git, .ssh) — operator-typo guard, not a security boundary. Does not import sibling Trinity modules (autonomy_governor, useful_compute_operator_loop, worker, payment scripts); reads JSON files only. Sprint 5.24's operator_loop hook integration verified untouched: cross-check safety test re-asserts that scripts/trinity/autonomy_governor.py did not gain any of its banned tokens in the course of 5.25. Tests: 34 new (15 functional + 12 schema + 7 safety) on trinity/governor-watchdog-v01, all green alongside the prior 1008 trinity tests. Pure scripts+schemas+docs+tests merge: zero src/, zero consensus changes, zero wallet/payment/broadcast changes. Explicit non-goals for v0.1 explicitly listed in docs/TRINITY_GOVERNOR_WATCHDOG_V01.md: no external dispatch even with --send, no long-running mode, no state between runs, no --fail-on flag (rc=0 always unless watchdog itself can't start), no Slack/PagerDuty/email integration — all reserved for the governor_watchdog_daemon.py process in a later sprint.
